topStories.headline: Suns end the Warriors' season, Magic rout the Hornets — the full 16-team playoff bracket is set
body: The final two playoff seeds were decided Friday night in decisive fashion. The Phoenix Suns jumped out to a 13–2 lead and never looked back, running the Golden State Warriors off the floor 111–96. Phoenix earns the West's No. 8 seed and will face the defending-champion, top-seeded Oklahoma City Thunder in Round 1. For Stephen Curry and the Warriors — who had clawed back from 13 down the night before to beat the Clippers — there is no next round.

In Orlando, the Magic were equally merciless, building a 35-point first-half lead on the Charlotte Hornets en route to a 121–90 blowout. LaMelo Ball's Hornets had been flying after their OT upset of Miami but were buried from the opening tip. Orlando is the East 8-seed and faces the Detroit Pistons in Round 1. The full 16-team playoff field is locked.

topStories.headline: Rockets at Lakers headlines a four-game Day 1 — Jokić opens at home, Knicks host Atlanta
body: Saturday is Day 1 of the NBA Playoffs with four first-round openers. The marquee attraction: Houston Rockets at LA Lakers (8:30 ET, ABC) — a prime-time showcase that pits two of the West's best against each other. Also today on Amazon: Toronto at Cleveland (1 ET), Minnesota at Denver (3:30 ET) where Nikola Jokić opens at home against Minnesota's elite defense, and Atlanta at New York (6 ET). Boston-Philadelphia and Detroit-Orlando tip off on other days to round out a loaded bracket.

topStories.headline: Stanley Cup Playoffs begin — Hurricanes, Stars, and the Battle of Pennsylvania tip off today
body: The Stanley Cup Playoffs open Saturday with three Game 1s across ESPN. The Carolina Hurricanes, East's top seed, host the Ottawa Senators at 3 ET. The Dallas Stars welcome the Minnesota Wild at 5:30 ET in a physical Central Division grinder. The most anticipated opener: Philadelphia Flyers at Pittsburgh Penguins (8 ET) — the Battle of Pennsylvania, with Sidney Crosby at 38 playing each postseason like it might be his last. The defending-champion Edmonton Oilers and Connor McDavid begin their title defense Sunday against Anaheim.

stories.headline: Phoenix demolishes Golden State — the Warriors are done, the bracket is locked
body: The Phoenix Suns raced to a 13–2 lead inside the first two minutes Friday night and cruised to a 111–96 victory over the Golden State Warriors, eliminating Golden State from the postseason. Phoenix earns the West's final playoff spot (No. 8 seed) and draws the defending-champion OKC Thunder in Round 1 — the toughest possible draw, but the Suns showed plenty of fight this week. For Stephen Curry and the Warriors, it's a second play-in exit in three years.

In Orlando, the Magic never let the Hornets breathe, building a 35-point first-half margin and winning 121–90. LaMelo Ball and Charlotte had been riding momentum after their OT upset of Miami two nights earlier, but they had no answers Friday. Orlando faces Detroit in Round 1. Full West bracket: OKC vs. Phoenix · Houston vs. LA Lakers · Denver vs. Minnesota. East: Cleveland vs. Toronto · Boston vs. Philadelphia · New York vs. Atlanta · Detroit vs. Orlando.

stories.headline: Four Game 1s today — Jokić opens at home, Rockets-Lakers on ABC closes the night
body: Toronto at Cleveland (1 ET, Amazon): The Cavaliers are heavy favorites at home with their depth advantage. Minnesota at Denver (3:30 ET, Amazon): Nikola Jokić vs. Rudy Gobert — a series that will likely hinge on interior dominance and transition defense. Atlanta at New York (6 ET, Amazon): The Knicks have home-court and Karl-Anthony Towns, but the Hawks are a resilient postseason opponent. And the night's marquee event: Houston at Los Angeles (8:30 ET, ABC) — LeBron James and the Lakers against a Rockets team that has been building toward this moment all season.

stories.headline: Three series open Game 1 today — Hurricanes, Stars, and the Battle of Pennsylvania
body: The Carolina Hurricanes are the East's top seed and one of the favorites to reach the Final. They host the Ottawa Senators at 3 ET (ESPN) — Ottawa edged into the playoffs on the final weekend and will be a massive underdog, but any series opening on the road in a playoff atmosphere is winnable. Dallas Stars vs. Minnesota Wild (5:30 ET, ESPN) is a Central Division battle built on checking lines and goaltending — Jake Oettinger vs. Marc-André Fleury is a compelling duel between generations.

The Battle of Pennsylvania — Philadelphia at Pittsburgh (8 ET, ESPN) — is the bracket's most emotionally charged series. Sidney Crosby, 38, plays each playoff run with the urgency of someone who knows the window is finite. The Flyers have been a younger, energized group all season. Meanwhile, the defending-champion Edmonton Oilers open their Stanley Cup title defense Sunday vs. the Anaheim Ducks, with Connor McDavid — Art Ross Trophy winner — the most dangerous player in the field.

stories.headline: Soroka wins his fourth, Diamondbacks beat Blue Jays 6-3 — Dodgers (13-3) lead the NL
body: Michael Soroka moved to 4–1 on the season — tied for the MLB lead in wins among starters — as the Arizona Diamondbacks beat Toronto 6–3 on Friday. Nolan Arenado homered and drove in three runs. The Pittsburgh Pirates have quietly gone 9–6 to sit atop the NL Central — one of the early-season surprises in the senior circuit. The Los Angeles Dodgers (13–3) remain the class of the NL with Shohei Ohtani's on-base presence anchoring the lineup. The New York Yankees have surrendered 18 runs over their past two games, raising real questions about their rotation depth. Saturday features a full 15-game MLB slate.

stories.headline: PSG vs. Bayern, Arsenal vs. Atlético — Europe's final four awaits April 28
body: PSG — who dismantled Liverpool 4–0 on aggregate — host Bayern Munich on April 28 at the Parc des Princes. It's the tournament's two most clinical sides: Ousmane Dembélé's pace against Harry Kane's finishing. Bayern's dramatic 6–4 aggregate win over Real Madrid was one of the UCL's great modern nights. On April 29, Atlético Madrid host Arsenal — Diego Simeone's defensive intensity against Mikel Arteta's organized press. Kai Havertz has been Arsenal's UCL clutch performer; Ademola Lookman is Atlético's danger man. Return legs: May 5–6. Final: Budapest, May 30.

stories.headline: Antonelli wins Japan, becomes history's youngest F1 championship leader — Miami is next
body: Kimi Antonelli won the Japanese Grand Prix at Suzuka and became the youngest driver in Formula 1 history to lead the Drivers' Championship, breaking Lewis Hamilton's record from the 2007 Spanish Grand Prix. The 18-year-old Mercedes rookie leads teammate George Russell by 9 points (72–63) after three races. Russell, who endured a difficult Japanese GP, will look to reset at Miami (May 1–3). Charles Leclerc (49 pts) keeps Ferrari in the fight; Hamilton (41 pts) remains a threat. The Mercedes intra-team duel is the season's defining sub-plot heading into the Americas swing.

stories.headline: Zverev into Munich semis, Alcaraz out of Barcelona — four tournaments reach their finish line Sunday
body: Alexander Zverev ground out a 5–7, 6–0, 6–2 win over Francisco Cerundolo to reach the Munich semifinals, where he faces Flavio Cobolli. In Barcelona, the ATP 500 semifinals feature Andrey Rublev vs. Hamad Medjedovic and Rafael Jodar vs. Lorenzo Musetti — the draw opened after Carlos Alcaraz withdrew from his home tournament. On the WTA side, the Stuttgart Porsche Grand Prix enters its final rounds with Aryna Sabalenka, Iga Swiatek, Coco Gauff, and Elena Rybakina all contending. All four clay events — Barcelona, Munich, Stuttgart, Rouen — conclude Sunday before the tour pivots to Madrid.

stories.headline: Simpson leads at -22 on moving day — Scheffler lurks two back at Harbour Town
body: Webb Simpson — a former RBC Heritage champion who has built a career around Harbour Town Golf Links — leads the field at -22 through three rounds. Scottie Scheffler and Stewart Cink are right behind at -19, with Justin Thomas and Matt Fitzpatrick at -17. Scheffler, coming off last week's Masters runner-up finish, is the most dangerous player within striking distance of any leaderboard. Ludvig Åberg, who opened with a bogey-free 63 to lead Round 1, will need a strong final round to threaten. Sunday's closing round at one of the PGA Tour's most scenic and beloved venues should produce a compelling finish.

stories.headline: AdventHealth 400 is Sunday — Reddick leads the points, Hamlin owns Kansas
body: The Cup Series moves to Kansas Speedway for the AdventHealth 400 on Sunday (2 ET, FOX) — Race 9 of 36. Tyler Reddick leads the championship standings and is consistently fast on 1.5-mile tracks. Denny Hamlin leads all active drivers with four career wins at Kansas — he's the man to beat whenever the series arrives in Kansas City. Kyle Larson, who dominated here last spring with 221 of 267 laps led, is the defending winner. Ty Gibbs, who snapped a 131-race winless drought with his 0.055-second photo finish at Bristol last week, arrives with the kind of confidence that can reshape a season.
